programmes.show_description: While Loretta takes Jodie back to the UK for her advert shoot, Rob tries to make use of her 5-star room, but the rest of the family have their own plans for its use. S9 E4

An unexpected visit coincides with Andy and Fergie's Royal Wedding. Alf Garnett has rivals for his affections. In Sickness and In Health S2 E6
Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap
David is carried away by the throb of guitars, the drone of the cicadas, the scent of the bougainvillaea, and most of all by Linda. S1 E3: Praying Mantis

Mr and Mrs Boswell pass on some heartfelt advice to their beloved daughter Aveline before she walks down the aisle. S5 E13

When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation while George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game
Classic sitcom. When Betty comes home after a dance lesson with their neighbour, Frank becomes suspicious of his intentions and insists on getting involved. S3 E3: Scottish Dancing

A heavily pregnant young woman turns up looking for Harold who she thinks is the father; he warms to the idea of becoming a dad, but it can only end in pain. S5 E4: Steptoe and Son - and Son!
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
Bob and Terry argue about fitness over a pint and challenge each other to a cross-country cycle race to see who's the fittest. S2 E5: The Great Race

As Christmas approaches Alf Garnett's problems increase - but things are eventually resolved by Caribbean magic. In Sickness and in Health Christmas Special 1986

programmes.show_name: Journeys: Isle of Man Steam Rail
programmes.show_description: Opened in 1873, the Isle of Man Steam Railway is the longest narrow gauge steam line in Britain that uses its original locomotives and carriages. We journey from Douglas to Port Erin.

With Jo gone for the weekend, Chrissy frets over being alone in the flat with Robin. S1 E4: And Then There Were Two

John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny faces a testing time as she thinks that she may be pregnant and has to face dinner with Vince's parents. S2 E6: Pregnant?
Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name
Classic sitcom featuring Frank Spencer, a well-meaning and enthusiastic one-man disaster area. Frank's grandad arrives from Australia to find his grandson in drag. S3 E4: Men as Women

Terry suggests he look after the house when Bob and Thelma go away to Scotland for the weekend, but he has an ulterior motive for making such a generous offer. S2 E6: Someday We'll Laugh about This
Since he can't sell his second hand cars, Arthur decides it might be a good idea to hire them out instead - but Ray has some trouble with the customers. S9 E10: Cars and Pints and Pains
Pauline is pregnant after an eventful murder mystery weekend, but who is the daddy? Cue the Oracle, who hones his sleuthing skills - Sir Henry, meanwhile, is still standing in for Joyce. S9 E6
